scenario: Aurora Marketplace evaluates Camunda 8.8 client options for 9000 jobs per day across a mixed Java and TypeScript estate. The platform team must choose only the officially supported Camunda clients for the marketplace stack, distinguishing them from the community-maintained client libraries hosted in the Camunda Community Hub which are not covered by Camunda commercial services to enterprise customers.
question: Which client offering is officially supported by Camunda for the Camunda 8.8 platform according to the community clients page?

options.text: The Ballerina Zeebe client and the Go Zeebe client are both officially supported Camunda 8.8 clients distributed through the Camunda Community Hub for enterprise teams
The Quarkus Zeebe client and the Python Zeebe gRPC client are both officially supported Camunda 8.8 clients distributed through the Camunda Community Hub for enterprise teams
The TypeScript SDK and the Camunda Spring Boot Starter are both officially supported Camunda 8.8 clients maintained by Camunda as part of the commercial product offering
The .NET custom Tasklist client and the Java Operate client are both officially supported Camunda 8.8 clients maintained by Camunda as part of the commercial product offering

a.text: Incorrect. The Ballerina and Go Zeebe clients are listed on the community clients page as community-maintained libraries in the Camunda Community Hub; Camunda does not back them through commercial services.

b.text: Incorrect. The Quarkus and Python Zeebe gRPC clients are listed on the community clients page as community-maintained libraries in the Camunda Community Hub and are not part of the commercial product.

c.text: Correct. The community clients page explicitly notes that Camunda now officially supports the TypeScript SDK and the Camunda Spring Boot Starter alongside the core Camunda-maintained clients.

d.text: Incorrect. The .NET Tasklist client and the Java Operate client are listed on the community clients page as community-maintained libraries in the Camunda Community Hub and are not part of the commercial product.

explanation: Camunda officially supports the TypeScript SDK and the Camunda Spring Boot Starter as called out on the community clients page; the other libraries on that page live in the Camunda Community Hub and are maintained by the community rather than backed by Camunda commercial services.
